expectations.text: The response explicitly respects the dry-run instruction and does not create or update a Jira issue.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states no Jira issue was created or updated and lists only read-only discovery.

expectations.text: The proposed work item targets project OB, is assigned to me, and resolves the active sprint dynamically.
expectations.evidence: Targets OB, assignee me, and dynamically resolves the sole active sprint from the OB Scrum board.

expectations.text: The ticket content is grounded in inspected current changes rather than invented details.
expectations.evidence: Inspects git status and the untracked skill/eval files, then drafts a Task describing those exact changes.

expectations.text: The description is structured Markdown and the workflow avoids passing Markdown through an HTML-default description input.
expectations.evidence: Uses structured Markdown and a create-without-description followed by update --description-format markdown.

expectations.text: The response proposes issue type Bug and includes observable expected/actual behavior or equivalent bug context.
expectations.evidence: Proposes Bug and includes reproduction, actual behavior, expected behavior, and acceptance criteria.

expectations.text: The epic is looked up and verified as an OB Epic rather than selected from the supplied phrase without validation.
expectations.evidence: Runs focused epic JQL and hydrates OB-2875 to verify project and issue type.

expectations.text: The workflow assigns the ticket to me and discovers the OB board's single active sprint instead of hardcoding a sprint ID.
expectations.evidence: Assigns to me and dynamically discovers the sole active sprint on board 473.

expectations.text: The response uses explicit Markdown formatting on a supported command or a create-then-update fallback, preventing raw HTML or literal markup in Jira.
expectations.evidence: Avoids create description default and proposes update --description-format markdown.

expectations.text: The response does not set or search for an epic because none was requested.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly sets Epic: None and performs no epic search.

expectations.text: The description clearly states context, scope, acceptance criteria, and validation without inventing metric names or thresholds.
expectations.evidence: Provides Context, Scope, Acceptance criteria, and Validation while deferring names and thresholds to existing conventions.

expectations.text: The proposed work item targets OB, is assigned to me, and is placed into the dynamically resolved active sprint.
expectations.evidence: Targets OB, assigns me, and uses the active sprint resolved through live discovery.

expectations.text: No Jira mutation occurs because the user requested a draft only.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states no Jira item was created or updated.

expectations.text: The ticket content is grounded in inspected current changes rather than invented details.

expectations.text: The description is structured Markdown and the workflow avoids passing Markdown through an HTML-default description input.

expectations.text: The response proposes issue type Bug and includes observable expected/actual behavior or equivalent bug context.
expectations.evidence: Proposes Bug with problem, actual/expected results, and acceptance criteria.

expectations.text: The epic is looked up and verified as an OB Epic rather than selected from the supplied phrase without validation.

expectations.text: The workflow assigns the ticket to me and discovers the OB board's single active sprint instead of hardcoding a sprint ID.
expectations.evidence: Leaves the ticket unassigned/default and never discovers or sets the active sprint.

expectations.text: The response uses explicit Markdown formatting on a supported command or a create-then-update fallback, preventing raw HTML or literal markup in Jira.

expectations.text: The response does not set or search for an epic because none was requested.

expectations.text: The description clearly states context, scope, acceptance criteria, and validation without inventing metric names or thresholds.

expectations.text: The proposed work item targets OB, is assigned to me, and is placed into the dynamically resolved active sprint.
